title: Rejected records and processing errors
summary: Explains how processing errors differ from normal backpressure and which metrics should trigger troubleshooting.

question_patterns: Is this a backpressure issue or a processing failure?

nodes.label: Processing errors
nodes.description: Errors encountered while collecting, transforming, or writing records.

nodes.label: Transformation work
nodes.description: The processing cost of mapping source records into intermediate JSON change events and any custom job output.

nodes.label: Processor throughput
nodes.description: The rate at which records complete read, transform, write, and acknowledgement steps.

nodes.label: Target write latency
nodes.description: The time needed to write transformed records to the Redis target database.

edges.condition: Records are rejected or processing failures prevent successful target writes.
edges.summary: Processing errors reduce successful throughput and should be investigated separately from normal backpressure.

edges.condition: Custom transformations or mappings require more processing per record.
edges.summary: More transformation work reduces effective processor throughput when CPU or task capacity becomes limiting.

edges.condition: Target writes are on the critical path for completed processing.
edges.summary: Slow target writes reduce the rate at which the processor can complete batches.

diagnostic_hints.interpretation: Rejected records or erroneous events indicate processing failures or data quality problems, not ordinary protective backpressure.
Custom transformation cost may be limiting processor throughput.

answering_guidance: Do not explain rejected records as normal backpressure.
Ask for or inspect processor logs when rejected records or erroneous events are present.
Separate transformation bottlenecks from target write bottlenecks by comparing transform/process metrics with write-time metrics.
